overview: This step aligns sentences within text units from two synchronized documents (source and target). It uses a character-length-based algorithm that accounts for language-specific expansion/contraction ratios, producing various match types (1-1, 2-1, 1-2, 0-1, 1-0, 2-0, 2-3, 3-2, etc.). Results can be output as a TMX file or as bilingual text units.

generateTMX.help:
Controls the output mode of the step:

- **Enabled (default):** The step generates a TMX file with aligned entries and returns the original events (possibly re-segmented) unchanged.
- **Disabled:** The step returns bilingual text units corresponding to the alignment (also segmented) instead of the original events.
notes: Currently, when TMX generation is enabled, the events sent by this step are the same as the events received — aligned text units are written only to the TMX file, not passed downstream. Returning aligned events inline is planned for a future version.

tmxOutputPath.help: Full path for the TMX output file. If the file already exists it will be **overwritten** without warning.
tmxOutputPath.values: A file system path ending in `.tmx`.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this path to be used

segmentSource.help:
Segments the source content before alignment. If the content is already segmented, existing segmentation is **replaced** with the new one.

If disabled, the source content is expected to be pre-segmented (e.g., by a prior Segmentation step).

useCustomSourceRules.help: Uses a specified SRX file for source segmentation instead of the built-in default rules.
notes: The default segmentation rules for this step are **hard-coded** within the step itself. They are **not** the rules from the `config` sub-directory of the Okapi installation.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for custom rules to take effect

customSourceRulesPath.help: Full path to an SRX document defining source segmentation rules.
customSourceRulesPath.values: A file system path to a valid `.srx` file.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this path to be used

segmentTarget.help:
Segments the target content before alignment. If the content is already segmented, existing segmentation is **replaced** with the new one.

If disabled, the target content is expected to be pre-segmented (e.g., by a prior Segmentation step).

useCustomTargetRules.help: Uses a specified SRX file for target segmentation instead of the built-in default rules.
notes: The default segmentation rules for this step are **hard-coded** within the step itself. They are **not** the rules from the `config` sub-directory of the Okapi installation.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for custom rules to take effect

customTargetRulesPath.help: Full path to an SRX document defining target segmentation rules.
customTargetRulesPath.values: A file system path to a valid `.srx` file.

dependsOn.condition: must be true for this path to be used

collapseWhitespace.help: Normalizes all whitespace characters (spaces, newlines, tabs, etc.) to a single space **before** segmentation and alignment are performed. Useful when source or target documents have inconsistent line breaks or extra spacing that would interfere with sentence boundary detection.

outputOneTOneMatchesOnly.help: Filters alignment output to include only 1-to-1 sentence matches, discarding any 2-1, 1-2, 0-1, 1-0, or other complex alignment types. Useful when you need high-confidence aligned pairs (e.g., for TM building).

forceSimpleOneToOneAlignment.help:
For each paragraph: if source and target have the **same number of sentences**, aligns them 1-to-1. If sentence counts differ, **joins all sentences** back into a single paragraph-level alignment.

This is a simpler, more conservative alignment strategy that avoids complex multi-sentence match types.
notes: When enabled, this **overrides** the *Output 1-1 matches only* option.

dependsOn.condition: overrides this when forceSimpleOneToOneAlignment is enabled

processingNotes: Source and target document text units **must be perfectly synchronized** (aligned at the paragraph level). If the source document has more text units than the target, an error is generated.
The alignment algorithm finds the best match based on character lengths, with internal parameters accounting for language-specific expansion/contraction ratios.
Entries marked as non-translatable are skipped and not processed.
When processing bilingual documents (e.g., TMX, PO), use only a **single input list**.

examples.title: TMX generation from parallel documents
examples.description: Align two parallel documents and produce a TMX file. Source and target are segmented using built-in default SRX rules.

examples.title: High-confidence TM building
examples.description: Use `outputOneTOneMatchesOnly` to extract only 1-to-1 sentence alignments for building a clean translation memory, discarding any ambiguous multi-sentence matches.

examples.title: Simple paragraph fallback alignment
examples.description: Use `forceSimpleOneToOneAlignment` for a conservative strategy: sentences align 1-to-1 when counts match, otherwise the entire paragraph is aligned as a single unit.
